summary: Fail loud when the assembly declares members/requirements but the gate resolves zero live members and zero lock declarations ‚Äî no more silent 'checked 0 / exit 0'

new.description: Drive the fail-loud coherence guard. (a) A workspace whose committed `temper.toml` declares `[[member]]`/`[requirement.*]` but resolves zero live members and carries no `lock.toml` declaration rows ‚áí an `error`-severity diagnostic and a non-zero exit ‚Äî the harness-root `temper check .` case the wave-end confirmation caught. (b) A correctly-rooted workspace that resolves ‚â•1 member does NOT fire (no false block on a clean gate ‚Äî law 3). (c) A genuinely empty harness (no `temper.toml` layer, no declarations) does NOT fire ‚Äî zero members is legitimate there. Prefer driving the pure predicate in `check.rs` directly; add a binary-level case if the existing CLI test harness makes it cheap.

edit.description: Add a pure, unit-testable predicate ‚Äî e.g. `fn empty_assembly_incoherence(declared: bool, resolved_members: usize, declarations_empty: bool) -> Option<Diagnostic>` ‚Äî that returns an `error`-severity `Diagnostic` (rule id e.g. `coverage.empty-assembly`) exactly when `declared && resolved_members == 0 && declarations_empty`. Message names the workspace root and the likely cause (a stale/absent `lock.toml`, or a mis-rooted workspace ‚Äî the harness root vs `./.temper`, or `emit` not run). Decouple from `AuthorLayer`/`Declarations` by taking primitives, so `main.rs` stays a thin call site. This does NOT gate on `temper.toml` requirement content (that read stayed retired at MANIFEST-MACHINERY-RETIRE) ‚Äî it is a coherence tripwire: the assembly declares work the gate then resolves none of.

In `gate()` (near the coverage-note tail, main.rs:888-898), compute the guard inputs and push its diagnostic. `declared` = the committed `layer` is `Some` and declares ‚â•1 member (document-carriage `[[member]]` or in-place) or ‚â•1 `[requirement.*]` ‚Äî via the `AuthorLayer` accessors (`members()`, `inplace_members()`, and a requirements accessor; add a small `requirements()`/`declares_any()` accessor on `AuthorLayer` in compose.rs only if none exists ‚Äî keep that additive). `resolved_members` = the sum of `member_counts` values. `declarations_empty` = the `declarations` read from `read_declarations` carries no requirement/satisfies/assembly/clause/kind rows. Extend `diagnostics` with the guard's `Option<Diagnostic>`. Place it so the correctly-rooted `temper check` (default `./.temper`, resolves 4 members) and a genuinely empty harness both stay silent.

tests.asserts: declared-but-zero-resolved + empty-lock ‚áí an error diagnostic and non-zero exit; a resolving workspace and a genuinely empty harness both stay silent

acceptance: `temper check` pointed at a harness whose committed assembly declares members/requirements but resolves none of them (no lock declarations, no live members) exits non-zero with a fail-loud `coverage.empty-assembly` error naming the root ‚Äî never the silent 'checked 0 members ... exit 0' the wave-end confirmation caught; a correctly-rooted check and an empty harness are unaffected.
notes: Wave-end confirmation: `temper check .` reads no `./lock.toml`/`./skills`, so members+requirements resolve empty and it exits 0 ‚Äî the silent-skip class temper exists to catch. This is the fail-loud safety net (error when `temper.toml` declares members/reqs but nothing resolves), not the producer/root restoration ‚Äî see the `(inplace-lock-producer)` fork, human-gated on the SDK-primary foundation.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels ‚Äî SDK npm packages, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle ‚Äî fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org + @temper scope, marketplace publish, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end; channel 1 (SDK publish) also rides the SDK-primary foundation

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution, 'install is two placements').

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery'). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering it. Scoped `@temper` names per the resolved `(project-name)` fork. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the SDK authoring-face manifest ‚Äî channel 1's `temper` core + `@temper/claude-code` provider module).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`temper` + `@temper/claude-code`, floors as exported clause arrays); ch2 = engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human creds.
